g.text: Why if [ 0 ]; then is ALWAYS true
if cmd; then → true when exit 0
The shell does NOT evaluate boolean expressions. It runs COMMANDS and checks their exit status. The [ command is just a program that performs tests and exits 0 (true) or 1 (false).

text.tspan: # 3. The [ command: tests if "0" is a non-empty string
# 5. Shell sees exit 0 → condition TRUE → runs then-body
# It tests: is the string "0" non-empty?
# 3. The [ command: tests if "0" is a non-empty string
# 5. Shell sees exit 0 → condition TRUE → runs then-body
# It tests: is the string "0" non-empty?

g.text: if true; then echo hi; fi
Flow: if true; then body; fi
